description:
Model a historic building with a Mansard roof and a central skylight in SketchUp Make 2017, then install a solar array on the concealed upper deck using the Skelion plugin.

Detailed Specifications:
1. Base Building: Create a 12m x 12m square footprint and extrude the walls to a height of 6m.
2. Mansard Roof: Above the walls, create a steep roof section that runs 1m inward from all sides and rises 2.5m in height. This should result in a 10m x 10m flat upper deck at a total height of 8.5m.
3. Skylight Obstruction: In the exact center of the 10m x 10m upper deck, draw a 2m x 2m square and extrude it upward by 0.5m to represent a central skylight.
4. Solar Panels: Select the remaining flat area of the upper deck (the ring around the skylight) and use Skelion to insert solar panels. Use standard panel dimensions (~1.6m x 1m), Landscape orientation, and Flush mount (0° tilt relative to the flat deck). Ensure no panels cover or intersect the central skylight.
5. Save Model: Save your completed file to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\mansard_firehouse.skp.
